segments.text: My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?
Why are you so far from saving me, so far from my cries of anguish?
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: Aijeleth…: or, the hind of the morning

segments.text: I cry out by day, but you do not answer,
by night, but I find no rest.
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: am…: Heb. there is no silence to me

segments.text: Yet you are enthroned as the Holy One;
you are the one Israel praises.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: In you our ancestors put their trust;
they trusted and you delivered them.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: To you they cried out and were saved;
in you they trusted and were not put to shame.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: But I am a worm and not a man,
scorned by everyone, despised by the people.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: All who see me mock me;
they hurl insults, shaking their heads.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: They say, 'He trusts in the LORD.'
'Let the LORD rescue him. Let him deliver him, since he delights in him.'
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: He trusted…: Heb. He rolled himself on
seeing…: or, if he delight in

segments.text: Yet you brought me out of the womb;
you made me trust in you, even at my mother’s breast.
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: didst…: or, kept me in safety

segments.text: From birth I was cast on you;
from my mother’s womb you have been my God.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Do not be far from me, for trouble is near
and there is no one to help.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Many bulls surround me;
strong bulls of Bashan encircle me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Roaring lions that tear their prey open their mouths wide against me.

footnotes: gaped…: Heb. opened their mouths against me

segments.text: I am poured out like water,
and all my bones are out of joint.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: My heart has turned to wax;
it has melted within me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: My mouth is dried up like a potsherd,
and my tongue sticks to the roof of my mouth;
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: You lay me in the dust of death.
Dogs surround me, a pack of villains encircles me;
they pierce my hands and my feet.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: All my bones are on display;
people stare and gloat over me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: They divide my clothes among them
and cast lots for my garment.
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: my darling: Heb. my only one

segments.text: But you, LORD, do not be far from me.
You are my strength; come quickly to help me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: I will declare your name to my people;
in the assembly I will praise you.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: You who fear the LORD, praise him!
All you descendants of Jacob, honor him!
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: For he has not despised or scorned the suffering of the afflicted one;
he has not hidden his face from him but has listened to his cry for help.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: From you comes the theme of my praise in the great assembly;
before those who fear you I will fulfill my vows.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: The poor will eat and be satisfied;
those who seek the LORD will praise him—may your hearts live forever!
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: All the ends of the earth will remember and turn to the LORD,
and all the families of the nations will bow down before him.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: For dominion belongs to the LORD
and he rules over the nations.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: All the rich of the earth will feast and worship;
all who go down to the dust will kneel before him—those who cannot keep themselves alive.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Posterity will serve him;
future generations will be told about the Lord.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: They will proclaim his righteousness, declaring to a people yet unborn:
'He has done it!'.
segments.speaker: David
